reviews.review_msg: 2 Midterms, 1 final, and and online assignments. This is a very tough class, and Shora does her best to make Microecon concepts easy to understand. This was the only class that i had to work my tail off, you will learn alot about economics and how it applies in the real world. This isnt an easy A class... but i would take her again in a heartbeat.

Shora was a great professor. Honestly, people who complain are the ones that don't try and expect a good grade. You just have to show up, take notes, and review her sample tests for both the midterms and final. She gave 9% for participation, tests worth 15% each, 3 assignments which helped on tests and increase grades !! and a final. Very helpful

Course was enviro econ so it may not reflect her in other courses. She cares about her students and teaches core concepts well. No real complaints -- tests were quite easy -- she doesn't hound you to make sure you are up to date -- so if you fail, it's really just from your own laziness -- even then, if you come to class and listen, you will pass.
Shora is amazing, she is such a nice marker. Truly cares about her students

At first, she seemed scary, strict, and uncompromising but turned out to be the exact opposite! The course was well structured, the expectations were clear, and she equipped us with everything needed to master all objectives. The lectures were terrific because she explained the material in a simple, straightforward manner.

Econ 201 is a hard class. Shora really tried to explain things in the easiest way possible, however you have to work a lot. This course needs a lot of work. Shora was always ready to help, after class or in office hours.

This is a hard class which requires to put effort on but overall she is a nice prof. She tries to explain everything clearly and if you have questions she is always available for help. Tutorial and assignments are easy. But u really need to study and understand course materials rather than memorizing things to do good.
Friendly prof. Knows what she is talking about. Easy exams. Nuff said.

Shora is a nice prof and obviously knows the material. Willing to hold extra office hours if you send emails to her. Although her hand-writing notes are bit messy, midterms are quite easy anyways. (Do the tutorial questions and you'll be fine).
She had extra office hours before the midterm. Very patient when I ask her questions. Even when I asked her questions like the exam ones, she answered patiently. Lots of real world examples.
Exams were very similar to homeworks. Very approachable. A very easy to pass class. Just read the notes and do the homeworks and sample exams.

Super understanding. Always available to help. Covered a lot of materials very fast, but I guess that was because we had three hour classes instead of two hour classes. Final was a joke, half of the questions were from practice problems she posted.

Shora is very friendly to talk with and she was always ready to help when I sent her emails or went to her office hours. The course was not the easiest course I have taken, but not the hardest one either. If you go to the lectures and solve the tutorial questions, you can pass the course easily and learn a lot. I highly recommend her class.
Dont do it to yourself. She does not help in any way and she does not teach. She reads off her slides that have many errors that end up confusing you more. Feedback? Whats that!

She ended a class with "I can see you are all very confused so we will end this here and I will see you next week". Taught 6 chapters in 2 classes(both were let out early), and then we had a mid term. She laughed at a student for not understanding.
